title: Agile Hardware Design — Krste Asanović
preview: Foundational lecture on agile hardware design methodology applied to open-source RISC-V processors and accelerators.
summary: Covers the principles of agile hardware design using RISC-V as a case study, including rapid iteration, open-source toolchains, and generator-based chip design. Discusses how academic and industry teams can produce competitive silicon faster by treating hardware like software projects.
datacenter: Relevant to custom accelerator development philosophy. Agile methods reduce time-to-silicon for AI accelerator prototypes and influence how datacenter operators evaluate ASIC vs GPU TCO.
metrics: Key result signal: demonstrates tape-out cycles measured in months rather than years through generator-based methodology.
